method: HENDRIX-Φ structured search operationalization via M32 + Yang-Mills curvature gating + phi gradient proposals

interpretation.phi_improvement: HENDRIX-Φ mean phi_resonance is +0.76% below linear scan.
interpretation.top_candidates: HENDRIX-Φ top-10% phi_resonance is +1.51% below linear scan — meaning its best nonce candidates are weaker search targets.
interpretation.domain_coverage: HENDRIX-Φ visits 32/32 M32 domains (+0 vs linear scan fewer).
interpretation.yang_mills_gating: HENDRIX-Φ mass gate pass rate: 98.7% (linear: 95.4%). Lower gate pass rate means the search is concentrating on low-curvature (mass-gapped) nonces.
interpretation.verdict: BASELINE: HENDRIX-Φ performs comparably to linear/random search at this scale. Structural advantages may emerge at larger search depths or with different hyperparameters.
